breaking_news.title: Trivy Supply Chain Attack Compromises CI/CD Pipelines with Credential Stealer
breaking_news.summary:
Threat actors known as TeamPCP compromised the Trivy vulnerability scanner, injecting credential-stealing malware into official releases and GitHub Actions workflows. This supply chain attack targeted thousands of CI/CD pipelines, with a self-propagating worm dubbed CanisterWorm spreading across 47 npm packages.

The breach forces organizations to treat all pipeline secrets as compromised and rotate them immediately to prevent further credential theft. This incident highlights the fragility of open-source security tools and the cascading risk to enterprise development environments relying on compromised scanners.

breaking_news.title: Oracle Patches Critical Unauthenticated RCE in Identity Manager
breaking_news.summary:
Oracle released an emergency patch for CVE-2026-21992, a critical unauthenticated remote code execution flaw in Identity Manager and Web Services Manager. The vulnerability carries a CVSS score of 9.8 and allows attackers to take full control of the system over HTTP without authentication.

Security researchers indicate the flaw may have already been exploited in the wild, posing a severe risk to organizations managing enterprise identity infrastructure. Immediate patching is strongly recommended to prevent unauthorized access and potential data exfiltration across affected systems.

breaking_news.title: Hackers Exploit CVSS 10.0 Flaw in Quest KACE SMA Systems
breaking_news.summary:
Arctic Wolf observed malicious activity consistent with the exploitation of CVE-2025-32975, a maximum-severity security flaw impacting Quest KACE Systems Management Appliance systems. Threat actors are actively targeting unpatched SMA systems exposed to the internet, with attacks detected starting the week of March 9, 2026.

The vulnerability is particularly dangerous for the education sector and enterprises relying on KACE for endpoint management, as it allows for complete system hijacking. Organizations must urgently apply patches to mitigate the risk of widespread compromise and potential data loss.

breaking_news.title: Russian Intelligence Targets Signal and WhatsApp Users in Phishing Campaign
breaking_news.summary:
The FBI and CISA warned that Russian intelligence-linked actors are conducting phishing campaigns targeting users of Signal and WhatsApp to compromise high-value accounts. Attackers pose as customer support services to hijack accounts belonging to government officials, military personnel, and journalists.

This campaign aims to seize control of encrypted messaging channels to access sensitive communications and contacts for intelligence gathering. The warning underscores the increasing threat of state-sponsored actors targeting commercial messaging platforms for espionage purposes.

breaking_news.title: WorldLeaks Ransomware Group Breaches Los Angeles Metro System
breaking_news.summary:
The WorldLeaks ransomware group breached the City of Los Angeles and its Metro system, forcing a shutdown of internal administrative computer systems. The attack disrupted station arrival displays and limited access to internal systems, impacting transit operations across the region.

While rail and bus services continued, the incident highlights the vulnerability of critical public infrastructure to ransomware attacks. The breach necessitated emergency responses and security checks to restore full access and ensure passenger safety.

breaking_news.title: CISA Adds Apple and Craft CMS Flaws to Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog
breaking_news.summary:
CISA added five security flaws impacting Apple, Craft CMS, and Laravel Livewire to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, ordering federal agencies to patch by April 3, 2026. The vulnerabilities include buffer overflows and code injection flaws with CVSS scores ranging from 7.8 to 10.0.

This directive follows reports of active exploitation in the wild, particularly affecting iOS devices and web applications. Federal agencies and private sector organizations are urged to prioritize patching to prevent unauthorized access and system compromise.

breaking_news.title: 7,500 Magento Sites Defaced in Global Hacking Campaign
breaking_news.summary:
A global hacking campaign defaced over 7,500 Magento sites since February 27, exploiting a critical REST API flaw named PolyShell. Attackers uploaded executable files without authentication, enabling remote code execution and account takeover on e-commerce platforms.

The campaign targeted global brands and government services, with defacement files placed across more than 15,000 hostnames. Adobe has released a fix, but many stores remain vulnerable due to unpatched configurations and older versions.

breaking_news.title: DoJ Disrupts 3 Million-Device IoT Botnets Behind Record DDoS Attacks
breaking_news.summary:
The U.S. Department of Justice, alongside Canadian and German authorities, disrupted command-and-control infrastructure for four major IoT botnets including Aisuru and Kimwolf. These botnets compromised over three million devices and were responsible for record-breaking DDoS attacks reaching 31.4 Tbps.

The operation targeted operators behind the botnets and seized domains to weaken their global cybercrime activities. This action significantly reduces the threat landscape for DDoS attacks against critical infrastructure and internet services.
